skill.name: plan-feature
skill.description: Plan a software feature by gathering requirements, reviewing architecture, creating tasks, and saving a Markdown plan for approval.
skill.summary: Create structured feature plans with requirements, architecture notes, tasks, risks, and open questions.
skill.author: DavidOpDeBeeck

security_audit.summary: Static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, code fences, hash-related wording, and feature examples. No shell execution, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection text was found in SKILL.md. The skill does intentionally instruct the agent to create a .claude/plans directory and write Markdown files, so publication should disclose scoped filesystem write behavior.

medium_findings.title: Scoped Filesystem Write Behavior
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to create .claude/plans and write a Markdown plan file derived from the feature name. This is legitimate for a planning workflow, but it modifies the workspace and should be visible to users before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is explicitly described in the instructions and output format. The risk is bounded because the destination is a local plans directory and the filename is slugified.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backticks and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The referenced lines contain file paths, task templates, or Markdown examples, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious syntax appears only in Markdown prose and examples. There is no script file, command invocation, or interpreter context in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography, but the reviewed lines contain ordinary planning language or examples such as feature descriptions and deck-related text. No cryptographic algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no crypto APIs, hashing implementation, encryption logic, or security-sensitive algorithm selection. The detected terms are prose within a planning template.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workspace File Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can cause the agent to create a directory and save a Markdown plan in the repository workspace. This is not malicious, but it is a side effect users should expect.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions directly require creating a directory and writing a plan file. The scope is local and specific, which lowers severity.

content.value_statement: Feature work often starts with unclear scope, hidden architecture risks, and weak acceptance criteria. This skill turns a request into a structured plan with tasks, risks, open questions, and an approval step.
actual_capabilities: Gathers feature goals, requirements, acceptance criteria, and open questions.
Guides review of existing code patterns, conventions, and integration points.
Structures architecture analysis across domain, application, adapter, and UI layers.
Creates atomic implementation tasks ordered by dependency.
Saves a Markdown feature plan under .claude/plans for later reference.
Presents the plan for approval before implementation begins.
limitations: It does not implement the feature by itself.
It depends on accurate access to the relevant codebase context.
It uses a repository-specific module model that may need adaptation.
It writes local plan files, so users should review workspace changes.

use_cases.title: Plan a Product Feature
use_cases.description: Convert a feature idea into requirements, acceptance criteria, affected modules, and a staged implementation plan.

use_cases.title: Prepare Architecture Review
use_cases.description: Identify integration points, design decisions, risks, and open questions before starting higher-risk development work.

use_cases.title: Create Onboarding Tasks
use_cases.description: Break a planned feature into clear development tasks that a new contributor can follow and verify.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a clear feature request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a feature to add saved filters to the dashboard. Include requirements, acceptance criteria, implementation tasks, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan With Codebase Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning after the assistant reviews nearby existing functionality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the plan-feature skill for adding team invitations. First inspect related user and organization code, then create a plan file.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Cross-Layer Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for backend and frontend changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a feature for custom notification preferences. Cover API contracts, persistence, UI state, tests, and migration risks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Before Approval
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning with explicit decision review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a plan for role-based access controls. Include key design decisions, rejected alternatives, dependency order, open questions, and wait for approval.

output_examples.input: Plan a feature that lets users customize voting decks.
output_examples.output: A Markdown plan with a feature summary, affected modules, design decisions, ordered tasks, risks, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Plan team invitation support after reviewing the current account code.
output_examples.output: A saved plan that names integration points, backend and UI work, acceptance criteria, and tests needed for invitation flows.

output_examples.input: Plan a safer timer reset workflow before implementation.
output_examples.output: A concise task breakdown that separates requirements, edge cases, validation rules, tests, and approval checkpoints.

best_practices: Provide the feature goal, user impact, and known constraints before invoking the skill.
Let the assistant inspect related files before it finalizes the task breakdown.
Review the saved plan and approve or revise it before implementation begins.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for product decisions that still need stakeholder input.
Do not skip review when the plan affects security, data models, or external integrations.
Do not treat the generated plan as final if the codebase context was incomplete.

faq.question: Does this skill write files?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the assistant to save a Markdown plan under .claude/plans in the workspace.

faq.question: Does it implement the feature?
faq.answer: No. It creates a plan and asks for approval before implementation starts.

faq.question: Can it work outside the original repository?
faq.answer: Yes, but module names and architecture sections may need adaptation for a different codebase.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the feature goal, expected users, constraints, and any known acceptance criteria.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the skill instructions.

faq.question: Why does it ask for approval?
faq.answer: The approval step helps confirm scope, design choices, risks, and task order before code changes begin.
